full_text_rationale: Full text describes Outcome-Conditioned Reasoning Distillation transforming historical same-repository resolved issues and verified patches into reusable procedural guidance for software-issue repair, with SWE-Bench Lite evaluation and temporal-leakage controls.
extractor_notes: Fresh search architecture extraction batch 012; controlled-comparison and quality status remain pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Chenglin Li, Yisen Xu, Zehao Wang, Shin Hwei Tan, Tse-Hsun Chen
titulo: Outcome-Conditioned Reasoning Distillation for Resolving Software Issues
formato_armazenamento: Repository-specific historical bug dataset storing resolved issues, verified patches, code context, affected locations, timestamps, and outcome-conditioned stage-wise repair reasoning plans; accepted exemplars are injected as structured guidance into repair stages.
framework_agente: O-CRD inference-time repair augmentation with repository-level exemplar mining, Exemplar Guardian compatibility filtering, Backward Reasoning Distillation, and Agentless execution scaffold
metodo_avaliacao: benchmark; leaderboard comparison; controlled scaffold comparison; difficulty-stratified analysis; Exemplar Guardian ablation; MCTS-style forward-search ablation; token and call overhead analysis
benchmarks_utilizados: SWE-Bench Lite with 300 real GitHub issues from 13 repositories
metricas_desempenho: Pass@1, file localization accuracy, function localization accuracy, difficulty-group Pass@1, McNemar significance test, LLM calls per issue, total tokens per issue
metricas_cl: Temporal-leakage-controlled historical exemplar reuse, same-repository exemplar retrieval, Exemplar Guardian stability, top-1 versus top-3 exemplar similarity, BRD versus MCTS reasoning-plan generation
baseline_comparado: Agentless, SWE-Agent, OpenHands, AutoCodeRover, MoatlessTools, MASAI, AutoCodeRover-v2, SWE-Search, O-CRD without Exemplar Guardian, O-CRD with MCTS-style forward search
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: For each of the 13 SWE-Bench Lite repositories, O-CRD builds a historical exemplar dataset from resolved bugs with verified patches that occurred strictly before the target issue; the paper does not report total memory-entry counts.
custo_por_tarefa: BRD averages 24.7 LLM calls and 14.3k total tokens per issue in the overhead ablation; the MCTS-style baseline averages 240 calls and 35.4k total tokens per issue.
reducao_tokens: Compared with the MCTS-style forward-search baseline, BRD uses 9.71x fewer LLM calls and 2.47x fewer total tokens per issue.
resultado_principal: On SWE-Bench Lite, O-CRD reaches Pass@1 of 37.7% with GPT-4o, 40.0% with GPT-5, and 35.6% with DeepSeek-V3.
desempenho_vs_baseline: With GPT-4o, O-CRD improves over Agentless from 27.3% to 37.7% Pass@1 and exceeds SWE-Search by 6.7 percentage points. With GPT-5, it improves Agentless from 29.7% to 40.0%; with DeepSeek-V3, from 27.0% to 35.6%.
custo_vs_baseline: In the reasoning-plan ablation, O-CRD with BRD achieves higher Pass@1 than the MCTS-style variant while using substantially fewer LLM calls and tokens.
limitacoes: The evaluation is limited to SWE-Bench Lite and depends on same-repository historical fixes; exemplar mismatch can cause negative transfer, and the cost comparison is against a controlled MCTS-style baseline rather than every possible search system.
ameacas_validade: Results depend on temporal filtering of historical issues, quality of verified patches, Agentless integration choices, LLM compatibility judgments, SWE-Bench Lite representativeness, and whether similar historical repairs exist for future repositories.
